subtitle: A teenage heart that carried a nation’s storm

panels.shot: dramatic low-angle mid-shot against a dusk sky
panels.action: Khudiram Bose stands in a simple dhoti and coat, shackled hands resting calm before him, gaze steady and unafraid, with a hazy colonial courthouse and distant gallows silhouetted behind in the fading light.

dialogue.text: Khudiram Bose, a boy of Bengal in 1908, walks from village dust to the gallows of Muzaffarpur with a calm that shakes an empire.

i18n.hi: १९०८ का बंगाल का छोरा ख़ुदीराम बोस, गाँव की धूल से चलते-चलते मुज़फ़्फरपुर के फाँसीघाट तक पहुँचता है, ऐसी शान्ति के साथ जो साम्राज्य हिला दे।

panels.action: Young Khudiram, barefoot and thin, walks along a dusty village path lined with date palms and small huts in Bengal, clutching a bundle of newspapers and leaflets as the evening sun glows behind him.

dialogue.text: He was born in a small village of Bengal in 1889, a child of fields and river winds, coming of age just as the Partition of Bengal tore through hearts and homes.

i18n.hi: वह १८८९ में बंगाल के एक छोटे गाँव में जन्मा, खेतों और नदी की हवाओं का बच्चा, ठीक तब जवान हो रहा था जब बंग-भंग ने दिलों और घरों को चीर दिया।

panels.shot: medium interior shot in a small town akhra
panels.action: Inside a lantern-lit room, Khudiram listens, eyes fierce and intent, as unseen elders address a gathered circle of youths; on a low table lie folded newspapers, a portrait of Bankim Chandra, and a worn copy of the Gita.

dialogue.text: In the akhras and secret circles of Bengal, he hears of Bande Mataram, of swadeshi, of a new creed: that some sons must give their lives for freedom before they have truly lived.

i18n.hi: बंगाल के अखाड़ों और गुप्त हलकों में वह ‘बंदे मातरम्’, स्वदेशी और एक नये धर्म की बातें सुनता है – कुछ बेटों को आज़ादी के लिए जान देनी होगी, जी भर के जीने से भी पहले।

panels.action: Khudiram sits cross-legged on a mat, head bowed over a leaflet bearing the words 'Partition', his jaw set in resolve, as the faint sound of a conch shell filters from a nearby temple.

dialogue.speaker: khudiram_bose
dialogue.text: If they can divide our land, must we not unite our courage?

i18n.hi: अगर वे हमारी ज़मीन बाँट सकते हैं, तो क्या हमें अपना जिगर नहीं जोड़ना चाहिए?

panels.action: In a secluded grove, Khudiram runs and practices with a wooden staff alongside other youths, his slight frame straining with effort while older men watch, the looming outline of a British police outpost seen far beyond the trees.

dialogue.text: Revolutionary leaders of Anushilan Samiti, and later those of Jugantar, draw in boys like him; they mix physical drills with vows of secrecy, and dream of striking at the empire with pistol and bomb.

i18n.hi: अनुशीलन समिति के क्रांतिकारी नेता, फिर आगे जाकर युगांतर वाले, ऐसे लड़कों को अपने में खींचते हैं; वे कसरत के साथ गोपनीयता की क़समें मिलाते हैं और पिस्तौल-बम से साम्राज्य पर वार के सपने बुनते हैं।

panels.shot: moody interior close shot over shoulder
panels.action: By the flicker of a lamp in a cramped room, Khudiram reads a handwritten note, his eyes widening at the name 'Muzaffarpur' and the mention of a British official, while a crude map lies beside him.

dialogue.text: In 1908, word travels through these hidden rooms: a plan is shaped to strike at Kingsford, the unpopular Sessions Judge transferred from Calcutta to Muzaffarpur, known for harsh sentences on nationalists.

i18n.hi: १९०८ में इन गुप्त कमरों में खबर फैलती है: किंग्सफ़ोर्ड पर वार की योजना बन चुकी है – वही बदनाम सेशन जज, जो कलकत्ता से मुज़फ़्फरपुर भेजा गया, राष्ट्रवादियों पर सख़्त सजाओं के लिए मशहूर।

panels.action: Under the weak lamps of a rural station at night, Khudiram, carrying a small cloth bundle, steps onto a train, glancing once at the dark countryside he is leaving behind, as a whistle blows in the mist.

dialogue.text: Khudiram and his comrade Prafulla Chaki are sent to Muzaffarpur, boys in simple clothes carrying hidden explosives, tasked with killing a judge under the shadow of law and empire.

i18n.hi: ख़ुदीराम और उसका साथी प्रफुल्ल चाकी मुज़फ़्फरपुर भेजे जाते हैं, साधारण कपड़ों में दो लड़के, छुपे बम लिए, क़ानून और राज की छाया में एक जज को मारने की ज़िम्मेदारी पर।

panels.action: On a dim Muzaffarpur street outside a European-style bungalow, Khudiram stands half-hidden under a tree, watching a horse-drawn carriage roll past, his hand tense at his side as the moon lights the judge’s residence beyond.

dialogue.text: For days they watch the judge’s routines, moving like shadows through the town, measuring distance, carriage, and courage, knowing one wrong throw could mean the life of another, or their own.

i18n.hi: कई दिनों तक वे जज की दिनचर्या पर नज़र रखते हैं, शहर में परछाइयों की तरह चलते, दूरी और बग्घी के साथ अपना दिल भी तौलते, जानते हुए कि एक ग़लत फेंक किसी और की या अपनी ही जान ले सकती है।

panels.action: On a roadside near Muzaffarpur on a dark April evening, Khudiram stands poised at the edge of the track, arm raised, as a carriage approaches fast; his face is taut between fear and grim decision.

dialogue.text: On 30 April 1908, they believe Kingsford rides in the approaching carriage; history records that the women inside are not the judge, but the wife and daughter of another British official, their lives about to be shattered.

i18n.hi: ३० अप्रैल १९०८ को उन्हें लगता है कि सामने आती बग्घी में किंग्सफ़ोर्ड सवार है; इतिहास दर्ज करता है कि भीतर जज नहीं, बल्कि एक और अफ़सर की बीवी और बेटी हैं, जिनकी दुनिया अब टूटने वाली है।

panels.shot: wide shot of aftermath at a distance
panels.action: From a distance, Khudiram is seen silhouetted against a sudden burst of light on the road as the bomb explodes; he turns to flee into the darkness while figures rush toward the stricken carriage.

dialogue.text: The bomb explodes, but Kingsford is not inside. Two Englishwomen, Mrs. and Miss Kennedy, are fatally injured. The act meant as justice becomes, instead, a tragedy that will stain the name of Bengal.

i18n.hi: बम फटता है, पर किंग्सफ़ोर्ड अंदर नहीं होता। दो अंग्रेज़ औरतें, मिसेज़ और मिस केनेडी, घातक रूप से घायल हो जाती हैं। न्याय के नाम पर किया गया वार, उल्टा, ऐसे त्रास में बदल जाता है जो बंगाल के नाम पर धब्बा छोड़ देगा।

panels.action: At dawn near a small station outside Muzaffarpur, a weary Khudiram, barefoot and dusty, walks alone along the track when armed constables appear, surrounding him as he straightens and meets their gaze without flinching.

dialogue.text: Khudiram and Prafulla flee in different directions. At Waini railway station, constables arrest Khudiram after a brief struggle; he is sixteen, perhaps seventeen, in chains, caught between boyhood and martyrdom.

i18n.hi: ख़ुदीराम और प्रफुल्ल दो दिशाओं में भागते हैं। वैनी रेलवे स्टेशन पर, हल्की झड़प के बाद सिपाही ख़ुदीराम को पकड़ लेते हैं; वह सोलह, शायद सत्रह साल का, हथकड़ियों में, बालपन और शहादत के बीच अटका हुआ।

panels.action: Inside a colonial police office, Khudiram sits on a wooden bench, wrists tied, as an English officer leans forward across a desk, questioning him; the boy’s face is calm, his posture upright despite the ropes.

dialogue.text: Interrogations follow. Documents from the Muzaffarpur and Alipore records speak of his reserve, his refusal to betray his comrades, and of the surprise of officers at this village boy’s composure under threat.

i18n.hi: फिर पूछताछें शुरू होती हैं। मुज़फ़्फरपुर और अलीपुर के कागज़ बताते हैं उसकी चुप-स्वभावी मज़बूती, साथियों को न बेचने की जिद, और अफ़सरों की हैरत – कि एक गाँव का छोरा धमकी के नीचे भी इतना सँभला हुआ कैसे है।

panels.shot: grand interior wide of colonial courtroom
panels.action: In the crowded Muzaffarpur Sessions Court, Khudiram stands in the dock, slight in his simple clothes, facing rows of British officials in wigs and Indian onlookers in turbans and dhotis, the ceiling fans stilling the hot air.

dialogue.text: The trial begins under the gaze of the Raj. The Muzaffarpur Sessions Court becomes a theatre of empire and youth, with lawyers, witnesses and policemen testifying, while the accused remains almost unnervingly calm.

i18n.hi: राज की कड़ी निगाहों में मुकदमा शुरू होता है। मुज़फ़्फरपुर सेशन कोर्ट साम्राज्य और युवावस्था का रंगमंच बन जाती है – वकील, गवाह, पुलिस गवाही देते हैं, और कटघरे में खड़ा लड़का लगभग डरावनी शान्ति से बैठा रहता है।

panels.shot: medium shot focused on Khudiram in the dock
panels.action: Khudiram grips the railing of the dock lightly, eyes level and steady, as Indian advocates argue before the judge; some spectators lean forward, watching this teenager as if he were older than the men around him.

dialogue.text: Contemporary accounts describe his poise: he listens attentively, answers questions without panic, the court struck by the contrast between his age and the enormity of the charge—murder by bomb, a weapon new and feared.

i18n.hi: समकालीन बयान उसकी ठहराव भरी चाल बताते हैं: वह ध्यान से सुनता है, बिन घबराहट सवालों के जवाब देता है; अदालत उसके उम्र और उस आरोप की हैसियत के फ़र्क से दंग रह जाती है – बम से हत्या, एक नया और दहशत भरा हथियार।

panels.shot: tight close-up on Khudiram’s face during questioning
panels.action: A close view shows Khudiram’s face beaded with sweat in the heat, yet his gaze remains unwavering toward the unseen judge, while a faint murmur from the packed gallery blurs behind him.

dialogue.text: From the Alipore Bomb Trial records we know his voice was clear, his statements measured; the British press itself noted his composure, asking what kind of spirit India was awakening in its sons.

i18n.hi: अलीपुर बम केस के रिकॉर्ड बताते हैं कि उसकी आवाज़ साफ़ थी, बयान नपे-तुले; अंग्रेज़ी प्रेस ने भी उसकी स्थिरता को लिखा, पूछती रही कि भारत अपने बेटों में कैसा जज़्बा जगा रहा है।

panels.action: The colonial judge sits high on the bench, lips set as he pronounces sentence; below, Khudiram stands straight in the dock, head slightly raised, while some Indians in the gallery press handkerchiefs to their faces.

dialogue.text: The court sentences him to death by hanging. Appeals and petitions follow, but the imperial machine moves on. Outside, newspapers carry his name from Muzaffarpur to Calcutta, from Bengal to the world.

i18n.hi: अदालत उसे फाँसी की सज़ा देती है। अपीलें और दरख़्वास्तें होती हैं, पर साम्राज्य की मशीन चलती रहती है। बाहर, अख़बार उसका नाम मुज़फ़्फरपुर से कलकत्ता, बंगाल से दुनिया तक फैला देते हैं।

panels.action: In the yard of Muzaffarpur Jail, Khudiram walks between two guards, chains at his ankles, yet his shoulders are relaxed and his eyes seem to look beyond the tall brick walls into the clear morning sky.

dialogue.text: Tradition says he smiled often in jail, that he spoke lightly with warders and walked to the gallows almost as to a festival; whatever the exact words, the memory of his calm spreads like whispered fire.

i18n.hi: कहानी कहती है कि वह जेल में भी अक्सर मुस्कुराता था, हवालदारों से हँसी-मज़ाक करता, और फाँसीघर की ओर ऐसे बढ़ा मानो किसी मेले में जा रहा हो; सटीक शब्द भले खो जाएँ, उसकी शान्ति की याद फुसफुसाती आग की तरह फैलती है।

panels.action: In a stark room of the jail, Khudiram stands in profile near the shadowed outline of the gallows platform, a rope hanging in the background; his face is turned toward an unseen window where a grey light filters in.

dialogue.text: On 11 August 1908, he is hanged in Muzaffarpur Jail, one of the youngest revolutionaries to face such an end. Outside, crowds gather, some praying, some silent, all sensing that a line has been crossed.

i18n.hi: ११ अगस्त १९०८ को, उसे मुज़फ़्फरपुर जेल में फाँसी दी जाती है, ऐसे सबसे नौजवान क्रांतिकारियों में एक, जिन्हें ऐसा अंजाम देखना पड़ा। बाहर, भीड़ जमा है – कोई प्रार्थना करता है, कोई चुप है, पर सब महसूस करते हैं कि कोई सीमा पार हो चुकी है।

panels.shot: montage-style wide shot of readers and listeners
panels.action: A composite scene shows a Calcutta street where people cluster around a newspaper seller holding up sheets with Khudiram’s name, while in a dim courtyard an old man reads aloud to young students who listen, faces pale and intent.

dialogue.text: The Bengalee and Amrita Bazar Patrika report his trial and execution; in homes and hostels, readers argue, mourn and wonder: what strength, and what blindness, drove a village boy to such a path?

i18n.hi: द बंगाली और अमृत बाज़ार पत्रिका उसके मुकदमे और फाँसी की खबर छापते हैं; घरों और हॉस्टलों में पाठक बहस करते, शोक मनाते, और पूछते हैं – कैसी ताक़त, और कैसी अँधियारी, एक गाँव के लड़के को इस राह तक ले आई?

panels.shot: symbolic slow pull-back from statue or portrait
panels.action: Years later, a modest statue of Khudiram stands in a small town square, garlanded with fresh marigolds, while schoolchildren look up at his youthful face, a passing tram and posters of non-violent leaders hinting at later chapters of the struggle.

dialogue.text: His story endures as both inspiration and warning: that courage, even in the smallest frame, can defy empires, but that without wisdom and care, the fire of patriotism can burn the innocent it seeks to save.

i18n.hi: उसकी कहानी आज भी एक प्रेरणा और चेतावनी दोनों है: कि हिम्मत, चाहे कितनी ही नाज़ुक देह में हो, साम्राज्यों को ललकार सकती है; पर यह भी कि बिना समझ और सावधानी के, देशभक्ति की आग उन्हीं बेगुनाहों को जला सकती है जिन्हें बचाने निकली हो।

subtitle_i18n.en: A teenage heart that carried a nation’s storm
subtitle_i18n.hi: एक नौजवान दिल, जिसमें उठी एक पूरी कौम की आँधी
